title: Except make or zapier, is there a way to send email based on subitem?
excerpt: Hello, I have already searched the old posts on Zapier/Make and workarounds regarding parent items status change (as a trigger). Are there any additional solutions for this feature? Many Thanks!

posts.author: kelvin_cmhahk (Kelvin)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello, I have already searched the old posts on Zapier/Make and workarounds regarding parent items status change (as a trigger). Are there any additional solutions for this feature? Many Thanks!

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Kelvin,

You’re right — monday.com doesn’t natively let you send emails directly from subitems. One thing that works for me is using mirror columns to bring subitem info up to the parent item, then triggering the email from there.

I’ve also been using Phonexa to help track and manage some of these automations — makes it easier to see which emails actually go out without losing any subitem info.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
@WirkKarl good call on using mirror columns - that’s a solid workaround.
If you’re looking for a simpler way to manage and send emails right from monday (without heavy Zapier/Make setups), check out Email to Tasks for monday.com . It lets you log and send emails directly from boards, keeping everything in sync with your items and subitems - super handy when dealing with status-triggered workflows.
